with.d: a word that shows something is together with something else
with.e: I went to the park with my friends.

was.d: a word that shows something happened in the past
was.e: I was at the park yesterday.

we.d: a group of people including the speaker
we.e: We are going to the park.

what.d: a question to ask about something

were.d: a past form of the word 'are'

when.e: When do you go to school?

who.d: a question to ask about a person

which.d: a question to ask about a choice
which.e: Which color do you like best?

would.d: a way to say something might happen
would.e: I would like to go to the park.

well.d: in a good way or to a good degree
well.e: I did my chores really well today.

want.d: to wish for or desire something
want.e: I want to play outside today.

where.e: Where do you want to go today?

way.e: There are many ways to get to school.

work.d: To do a job or task
work.e: The students worked hard on their science project.

world.d: The entire planet we live on
world.e: People from all over the world came to the festival.

why.d: the reason or cause for something
why.e: Why do we have to go to bed early tonight?

while.e: I read a book while waiting for the bus.

without.e: I can't play outside without my coat.

women.e: The women in my family are very kind.

water.d: A clear liquid that we drink and use for cleaning
water.e: I drink a glass of water every day.

went.e: I went to the park with my friends.

week.e: I have soccer practice three times a week.

war.d: A fight between countries or groups
war.e: The soldiers went to war to protect our country.

whole.e: I ate the whole cookie because it was so good.

white.d: the color of snow and milk
white.e: The swan's feathers are all white.

within.d: inside or in the middle of something
within.e: The cat is sleeping within its basket.

working.e: I am working on my math homework.

wrong.e: I think I solved the math problem the wrong way.

wait.d: to stay in one place until something happens
wait.e: I need to wait patiently for my turn on the swings.

whether.e: I'm not sure whether it will rain today.

win.d: to be the best and get a prize
win.e: The team worked hard and was able to win the game.

wanted.d: To wish for or desire something
wanted.e: The child wanted a new toy for their birthday.

woman.e: The kind woman helped the lost puppy find its way home.

watch.d: To look at something carefully for a period of time
watch.e: I watch my pet dog play in the yard.

works.d: To do a job or task
works.e: My mom works at the hospital.

word.d: A set of letters that has a special meaning

west.d: The direction where the sun sets
west.e: I live in the west part of the city.

words.d: The smallest unit of language that has meaning
words.e: The teacher taught us new words to use in our writing.

wife.e: My mom is the wife of my dad.

wants.d: To wish for or desire something
wants.e: The little girl wants a new bike for her birthday.

wish.d: To hope for something to happen
wish.e: I wish I could go to the park today.

won.d: To be successful in a competition or game
won.e: My soccer team won the championship game!

worked.d: To do a job or task
worked.e: I worked hard on my homework to get a good grade.

weeks.e: I have to wait two weeks for my birthday.

whatever.d: Used to talk about something in a general or non-specific way.
whatever.e: I'll wear whatever clothes I find in my closet.

whose.d: Asking about something that belongs to someone.
whose.e: Whose book is left on the desk?

worth.d: How valuable or important something is.
worth.e: That necklace is worth a lot of money.

website.d: A place on the internet to find information
website.e: I like looking at my favorite website.

written.d: Something that is put down on paper
written.e: The story was written by a famous author.

ways.d: Different methods or paths to do something
ways.e: There are many ways to get to the park.

waiting.d: Being ready and expecting something to happen.
waiting.e: I waited patiently for my mom to come home from work.

walk.d: To move by putting one foot in front of the other.
walk.e: I like to walk to school every day.

writing.d: The act of making letters or words on paper.
writing.e: My teacher taught me how to do good writing.

wrote.d: The past tense of 'write', which means to make letters or words.
wrote.e: I wrote a story for my English class.

workers.d: People who have a job and do work
workers.e: The construction workers are building a new house

western.d: Coming from or belonging to the west
western.e: The cowboy rode off into the western sunset.

wanna.e: I wanna go to the park and play on the swings.

weight.e: The kitten is a small weight compared to the big dog.

wall.d: The tall, hard surface that goes around a room or building
wall.e: The painting is hanging on the wall of my bedroom.

welcome.d: When you are happy to have someone come
welcome.e: You are welcome to join us for the party.

write.d: To use a pen or pencil to make letters on paper
write.e: I will write my name at the top of the page.

washington.d: The name of a famous president of the United States
washington.e: George Washington was the first president of the US.

watching.d: Looking at and paying attention to something
watching.e: The kids were watching the movie.

wow.d: An expression of surprise or amazement

wide.d: having a lot of space from one side to the other
wide.e: The road was wide enough for two cars to pass.

whom.d: The person that something is done to or given to.
whom.e: I'm not sure to whom I should give this letter.

winning.d: being the best or getting first place in a competition
winning.e: The soccer team was excited about winning the big game.

winter.d: the cold season of the year when it sometimes snows
winter.e: In the winter, the ground is covered with a blanket of snow.

worst.e: The thunderstorm was the worst I had ever seen.

weather.d: The temperature, rain, wind, and other conditions outside
weather.e: The weather is sunny today, so we can play outside.

wonder.d: To be curious about something and want to know more
wonder.e: I wonder what kind of animals live in the forest.

wear.d: To have clothing on your body.
wear.e: I wear my favorite shirt to school.

worse.d: Not as good as something else.
worse.e: The weather is worse today than it was yesterday.

weekend.d: The two days between Friday and Monday when many people do not have to work
weekend.e: My family always goes on fun outings during the weekend.

wind.d: The movement of air, often strong enough to blow things around
wind.e: On a windy day, I like to fly my kite in the park.

walking.d: Moving around by putting one foot in front of the other
walking.e: The dog enjoyed walking with its owner.

windows.d: The glass openings in the walls of a building
windows.e: The sun shines through the windows.

worry.d: To feel afraid or concerned about something
worry.e: The student worried about the big test.

william.e: William likes to play soccer with his friends.

wearing.e: I am wearing my favorite shirt today.

weird.d: Something that is strange or unusual
weird.e: The new kid at school has some weird habits.

window.d: The glass part of a wall you can see through
window.e: I like to look out the window and watch the birds.

web.d: A big set of websites on the internet
web.e: I searched the web to find information for my school project.

wild.d: Not tame or living in nature
wild.e: The deer in the forest are wild animals.

weapons.d: Tools used to hurt or fight against others
weapons.e: The soldier carried powerful weapons to protect the town.

waste.d: To use too much of something or to not use something properly
waste.e: I should not waste water by leaving the faucet running.

wood.d: A hard material from trees that is used to make things
wood.e: The table in my kitchen is made of strong wood.

wonderful.d: Something that is very good or pleasing
wonderful.e: The flowers in the garden look wonderful today.

wake.d: To become conscious and start the day.
wake.e: I always wake up early to eat breakfast.

winner.d: The person who wins a game or contest.
winner.e: The winner of the race got a trophy.

warm.d: Something that feels nice and cozy, like a hug or the sun
warm.e: I love to curl up in a warm blanket on the couch.

wedding.d: A special event where two people get married and become husband and wife.
wedding.e: My cousin is having a big wedding this summer.

willing.d: Wanting to do something and doing it without being forced.
willing.e: I am willing to help you with your homework.

watched.d: to look at or observe something carefully
watched.e: I watched the birds flying in the sky.

weak.e: The baby bird was too weak to fly out of the nest.

walked.d: To move your feet to get from one place to another
walked.e: The boy walked to the park.

wins.d: To be the best in a game or competition
wins.e: The team wins the soccer game.

writer.d: A person who writes stories, articles, or books.
writer.e: The famous writer wrote an exciting new book.

williams.e: My friend's last name is Williams.

walls.d: The sides of a room or building
walls.e: The walls of the house are painted white.

whenever.d: at any time that something happens
whenever.e: I can play outside whenever the weather is nice.

worried.d: Worried means to feel anxious or uneasy about something.
worried.e: I was worried I would be late for my dentist appointment.

warning.d: A warning is a message that tells you something dangerous or bad might happen.
warning.e: The teacher gave a warning to the students to be quiet.

wave.d: A wave is when water moves up and down in the ocean or a lake.
wave.e: The big wave crashed onto the beach.

wars.d: A fight between countries or groups of people.
wars.e: The two countries had a long war that lasted many years.

wing.d: The part of a bird that helps it fly.
wing.e: The bird used its strong wings to soar high in the sky.

wales.d: A country that is part of the United Kingdom
wales.e: My family visited Wales on vacation last summer.

widely.d: Existing or happening over a large area
widely.e: The news was reported widely across the country.

wednesday.d: The fourth day of the week, after Tuesday and before Thursday.
wednesday.e: We have a field trip on Wednesday.

witness.e: The witness told the police what she saw.

weapon.d: Something used to hurt or fight with.
weapon.e: The soldier used a weapon to protect the town.

wealth.d: a lot of money or valuable things
wealth.e: The family's wealth allowed them to take a nice vacation.

wheel.d: a round object that helps a vehicle move
wheel.e: The car's wheels made it easy to drive down the road.

worldwide.d: happening or existing all over the world
worldwide.e: The toys were sold worldwide in many different countries.

waters.d: The liquid that is in oceans, lakes, and rivers
waters.e: They played in the waters of the lake.

wet.d: When something is full of water or other liquid
wet.e: I got my shoes wet when I played in the rain.

wilson.d: A person's name, like the name of a boy or a girl
wilson.e: My neighbor's name is Mr. Wilson.

wise.d: When someone is very smart and makes good choices
wise.e: Grandma is a wise old lady.

writers.d: People who write books, stories, or articles
writers.e: The famous writers held a signing event at the bookstore.

weekly.e: I have a weekly piano lesson on Wednesdays.

worker.e: The construction worker is building a new house.

whereas.d: Used to introduce a contrasting idea or situation.
whereas.e: The boy liked chocolate ice cream, whereas his sister preferred vanilla.

wondering.d: Thinking about something and trying to figure it out.
wondering.e: The little girl was wondering what kind of present she would get for her birthday.
